# o7meaning.com

> Plain-English explanation of "o7" — the text emoticon salute used across gaming communities, where the "o" is a head and the "7" a raised arm. Covers what o7 means, where it came from, and how it is used on Twitch, Discord, EVE Online, and especially Star Citizen. A fan-made community resource by Doc_Flanigan. Not affiliated with or endorsed by Cloud Imperium Games or Roberts Space Industries.

## Pages

- [What Does o7 Mean?](https://o7meaning.com/): The gaming salute explained — the "o" is a head, the "7" a raised arm, used across Twitch, Discord, EVE Online, and Star Citizen.
- [o7 in Star Citizen](https://o7meaning.com/in-star-citizen): How o7 is used as the universal greeting between Star Citizen players, when to use it, and how to get started in the game.
- [FAQ](https://o7meaning.com/faq): Answers to common questions about o7 — what it means, its origins, and how it is used in Star Citizen and other games.

## Key facts

- o7 is a text emoticon that represents a person saluting: the "o" is the head and the "7" is a raised arm.
- It is used as a sign of respect, greeting, or farewell across many gaming communities, including Twitch, Discord, and EVE Online.
- In Star Citizen, o7 is a common greeting and sign of respect exchanged between players.
- The emoticon is typed as a lowercase letter "o" followed by the number "7".

## Related

- [dayonecitizen.com](https://dayonecitizen.com): Plain-English getting-started guide for brand-new Star Citizen players.
- [iheldtheline.com](https://iheldtheline.com): Squadron 42 release-date tracking, developer news, and FAQ.
